is the head of government in the state of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). The [Government of New South Wales](/wiki/Government_of_New_South_Wales "Government of New South Wales") follows the [Westminster Parliamentary System](/wiki/Westminster_system "Westminster system"), with a [Parliament of New South Wales](/wiki/Parliament_of_New_South_Wales "Parliament of New South Wales") acting as the legislature. The premier is appointed by the [Governor of New South Wales](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales"), and by modern convention holds office by their ability to command the support of a majority of members of the lower house of Parliament, the [Legislative Assembly](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Legislative_Assembly "New South Wales Legislative Assembly").

The **premier of New South Wales** is the head of government in the state of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). The [Government of New South Wales](/wiki/Government_of_New_South_Wales "Government of New South Wales") follows the [Westminster Parliamentary System](/wiki/Westminster_system "Westminster system"), with a [Parliament of New South Wales](/wiki/Parliament_of_New_South_Wales "Parliament of New South Wales") acting as the legislature. The premier is appointed by the [Governor of New South Wales](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales"), and by modern convention holds office by their ability to command the support of a majority of members of the lower house of Parliament, the [Legislative Assembly](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Legislative_Assembly "New South Wales Legislative Assembly").

Before [Federation](/wiki/Federation_of_Australia "Federation of Australia") in 1901, the term "**prime minister of New South Wales**" was also used. "Premier" has been used more or less exclusively from 1901, to avoid confusion with the federal [prime minister of Australia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Australia "Prime Minister of Australia").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The current premier is [Chris Minns](/wiki/Chris_Minns "Chris Minns"), the leader of the [New South Wales Labor Party](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Labor_Party "New South Wales Labor Party"), who assumed office on 28 March 2023. Minns defeated [Dominic Perrottet](/wiki/Dominic_Perrottet "Dominic Perrottet") at the election held on 25 March 2023, after twelve years of [Liberal/National Coalition](/wiki/Coalition_(Australia) "Coalition (Australia)") rule.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
